title: C4 conformance-vector format + seed corpus
manager: The conformance-vector format — a document plus a seed plus stimuli, producing a canonical trace and digest — and the first corpus of vectors, seeded by promoting M3's determinism tests. This is the executable definition of "correct FSL behavior": every normative feature ships a vector, and any implementation that produces a different trace fails. It is the artifact that makes "portable" checkable rather than aspirational, and it cannot precede C2 because the trace events are named by the operational semantics.
cs: schema `fsl.vector.v1` (document, seed, stimuli -> canonical trace + digest); seeded by promoting M3 determinism tests; standing rule: no normative feature without a vector; thin-harness contract; depends on C2 (WP6.6) for trace-event names and C1 (W6.99) for the digest.
